title: scrophularia aquatica subsp. oblongifolia
intro: Scrophularia aquatica subsp. oblongifolia, also known as water figwort or scorpion root, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the figwort family Scrophulariaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Scrophularia aquatica subsp. oblongifolia include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting the immune system. The plant is used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures, teas, and infusions, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
